rulings.Case Title: FRANK HENRY TANNER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Domco Products Texas, Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs¿ Product Identification Witnesses¿ Depositions; And Motion For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Domco Products Texas Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiffs' product identification witnesses' deposition and for sanctions is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS KING VS. CRANE CO., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continuance Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant Black Swan Manufacturing Co. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 29, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Plaintiff's motion to compel defendant Black Swan Manufacturing Co. to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition is granted. Plaintiff properly noticed defendant's deposition, defendant did not file objections, defendant did not substantively oppose this motion nor appear at the initially scheduled hearing date and there has been no demonstration why this Court should not issue an order compelling the deposition and document production. The deposition must commence no later than June 6, 2014 with documents to be produced at the deposition. Plaintiff's request for sanctions of $1,061 is granted. (C.C.P. 2025.450(g).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
